title: Computed
description: The #[Computed] attribute allows you to create derived properties that are cached during a request, providing a performance advantage when accessing expensive operations multiple times.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
Apply the #[Computed] attribute to any method to turn it into a cached property:
The user() method is accessed like a property using $this->user. The first time it's called, the result is cached and reused for the rest of the request.
Unlike normal properties, computed properties must be accessed via $this in your template. For example, $this->posts instead of $posts.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/user/⚡show.blade.php use Livewire\Attributes\Computed;use Livewire\Component;use App\Models\User; new class extends Component { public $userId;  #[Computed] public function user() { return User::find($this->userId); }  public function follow() { Auth::user()->follow($this->user); }};

sections.title: #Performance advantage
sections.content:
Computed properties cache their result for the duration of a request. If you access $this->posts multiple times, the underlying method only executes once:
This allows you to freely access derived values without worrying about performance implications.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/post/⚡index.blade.php use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Auth;use Livewire\Attributes\Computed;use Livewire\Component; new class extends Component { #[Computed] public function posts() { return Auth::user()->posts; // Only queries database once }};

sections.title: #Busting the cache
sections.content:
If the underlying data changes during a request, you can clear the cache using unset():
After creating a new post, unset($this->posts) clears the cache so the next access retrieves the updated data.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/post/⚡index.blade.php use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Auth;use Livewire\Attributes\Computed;use Livewire\Component; new class extends Component { #[Computed] public function posts() { return Auth::user()->posts; }  public function createPost() { if ($this->posts->count() > 10) { throw new \Exception('Maximum post count exceeded'); }  Auth::user()->posts()->create(...);  unset($this->posts); // Clear cache }};

sections.title: #Caching between requests
sections.content:
By default, computed properties only cache within a single request. To cache across multiple requests, use the persist parameter:
This caches the value for 3600 seconds (1 hour). You can customize the duration:

examples.code: #[Computed(persist: true)] public function user(){ return User::find($this->userId);}

sections.title: #Caching across all components
sections.content:
To share cached values across all component instances in your application, use the cache parameter:
You can optionally set a custom cache key:

examples.code: use Livewire\Attributes\Computed;use App\Models\Post; #[Computed(cache: true)] public function posts(){ return Post::all();}

sections.title: #When to use
sections.content: Computed properties are especially useful when:

sections.title: #Limitations
sections.content: Computed properties cannot be used on Livewire\Form objects. Attempting to access them via $form->property will result in an error.

sections.title: #Learn more
sections.content: For detailed information about computed properties, caching strategies, and advanced use cases, see the Computed Properties documentation.

examples.code: #[Computed( bool $persist = false, int $seconds = 3600, bool $cache = false, ?string $key = null, mixed $tags = null,)]
